content:
# Quarterly Knowledge Audit Checklist

**Tool ID:** T080-05
**Block:** B080 (Knowledge Capture System)
**Domain:** Team & Talent (D04_team_talent)
**Level:** L3
**Format:** Checklist
**Role:** Manager
**Time Required:** 20-30 minutes
**Version:** 1.0

---
## Welcome

This is a culture-level tool. It helps you move from "good enough" to genuine excellence -- the kind your team takes pride in and your guests can feel. If your best barista left tomorrow, would the cafe still run the same way? This audit tells you.

Not another tick-box exercise. This checklist exists because the details it covers are the ones that slip when you are busy -- and the ones that matter most when they do.

**Built for Manager working in Team & Talent.**
Use it. Adapt it. Make it yours.

---

## Purpose

Structured checklist for quarterly review of the Operations Manual covering document currency, identified gaps, new procedures needing documentation, and team feedback on usability. This ensures your cafe's knowledge base stays alive and accurate rather than gathering dust.

This tool supports **Knowledge Capture System** (B080) and is designed to be practical, printable, and immediately useful in a working cafe environment.

---

## How to Use This Tool

**Who:** Manager
**Time Required:** 20-30 minutes
**Frequency:** Quarterly (January, April, July, October). Also after major changes (new equipment, menu overhaul, new supplier).

**What You Will Need:**
- This checklist (printed or tablet)
- Access to the Operations Manual / SOP folder
- T080-04 Change Log Tracker (for cross-reference)
- Input from Head Barista and Kitchen Lead (ask them before the audit)
- List of any changes made in the last quarter

**Steps:**
1. Before the audit: ask the team "Is anything in the manual wrong or missing?"
2. Open the Operations Manual index (T080-03) and work through section by section
3. Check each document against the criteria below
4. Mark status and note any issues
5. Create an action list for updates needed
6. Assign owners and deadlines for each action
7. Update T080-04 Change Log with all findings

---

## Checklist

### Section 1: Document Currency (Is everything up to date?)

| # | Check Item | Pass | Fail | N/A | Details |
|---|-----------|------|------|-----|---------|
| 1.1 | All SOPs reviewed within last 12 months (check review dates) | P | F | | Last unreviewed SOP: _____ |
| 1.2 | Espresso recipe card matches current coffee and machine settings | P | F | | Current recipe: _____g in / _____g out / _____s |
| 1.3 | Allergen matrix matches current menu (including specials) | P | F | | Last updated: _____ |
| 1.4 | Cleaning schedule reflects current chemical products and dilutions | P | F | | |
| 1.5 | Supplier contact list is current (phone, email, delivery days) | P | F | | Changed suppliers since last audit? Y/N |
| 1.6 | Staff training records match current team roster | P | F | | Leavers removed? New starters added? |
| 1.7 | Opening/closing checklists match current equipment and layout | P | F | | Any new equipment since last audit? |
| 1.8 | Emergency procedures match current contacts and locations | P | F | | Fire exits unchanged? First aiders current? |

**Section 1 Score:** _____ / 8 Pass

### Section 2: Gap Identification (What is missing?)

| # | Check Item | Pass | Fail | N/A | Details |
|---|-----------|------|------|-----|---------|
| 2.1 | Any new procedures introduced in the last quarter WITHOUT documentation? | P | F | | Describe: _____ |
| 2.2 | Any new equipment added without an operating/cleaning procedure? | P | F | | Equipment: _____ |
| 2.3 | Any supplier changes without updated spec sheets or allergen info? | P | F | | Supplier: _____ |
| 2.4 | Any workarounds or "the way we actually do it" that differ from the SOP? | P | F | | Describe: _____ |
| 2.5 | Any knowledge held by only one person? (bus factor = 1) | P | F | | Person: _____ Knowledge: _____ |
| 2.6 | Any customer complaints that revealed a missing procedure? | P | F | | Complaint: _____ |

**Section 2 Score:** _____ / 6 Pass

### Section 3: Usability and Team Feedback

| # | Check Item | Pass | Fail | N/A | Details |
|---|-----------|------|------|-----|---------|
| 3.1 | Team members know where to find the Operations Manual | P | F | | Ask 2 staff: "Where is the manual?" |
| 3.2 | Team members have used the manual in the last month | P | F | | Ask 2 staff: "When did you last look at it?" |
| 3.3 | Documents are easy to understand (clear language, no jargon) | P | F | | Ask: "Is anything confusing?" |
| 3.4 | Documents are in a logical order / easy to find specific topics | P | F | | |
| 3.5 | Physical copies (if used) are clean, readable, not torn or stained | P | F | | |
| 3.6 | Digital copies (if used) are accessible from the shop floor | P | F | | |

**Section 3 Score:** _____ / 6 Pass

### Section 4: Change Log Verification

| # | Check Item | Pass | Fail | N/A | Details |
|---|-----------|------|------|-----|---------|
| 4.1 | T080-04 Change Log has been updated for all changes this quarter | P | F | | Missing entries: _____ |
| 4.2 | All logged changes have been communicated to the team | P | F | | How communicated: _____ |
| 4.3 | Version numbers / dates on documents match Change Log entries | P | F | | Mismatches: _____ |

**Section 4 Score:** _____ / 3 Pass

### Overall Audit Summary

| Section | Score | Maximum | Status |
|---------|-------|---------|--------|
| 1. Document Currency | | 8 | |
| 2. Gap Identification | | 6 | |
| 3. Usability | | 6 | |
| 4. Change Log | | 3 | |
| **TOTAL** | | **23** | |

**Rating:**
- 21-23 Pass: Excellent -- knowledge base is current and used
- 17-20 Pass: Good -- minor updates needed, schedule within 2 weeks
- 12-16 Pass: Needs Attention -- significant gaps, schedule update sprint
- Below 12: Critical -- knowledge base is not serving its purpose, escalate to Owner

### Actions Required

| Priority | Document / Gap | Action Needed | Owner | Deadline | Status |
|----------|---------------|---------------|-------|----------|--------|
| 1 | | | | | |
| 2 | | | | | |
| 3 | | | | | |
| 4 | | | | | |
| 5 | | | | | |

**Completion:**
- Audit date: _______________
- Auditor: _______________
- Overall score: _____ / 23
- Next audit due: _______________
- Signed: _______________

---

## Common Risks

### Risk 1: The Manual Exists But Nobody Uses It

**Why it happens:** Documents were created during a compliance push, filed neatly, and never opened again. Team relies on verbal knowledge and habit.
**How to prevent:** Reference the manual actively. When a team member asks "how do we do X?", reply "let me show you where that is in the manual" instead of just telling them.
**If it is already happening:** Pick one SOP that is frequently relevant (e.g., allergen query procedure). Print it, laminate it, put it at the station where it is needed. Start with one.

### Risk 2: Key Knowledge Lives in One Person's Head

**Why it happens:** The Head Barista knows the grinder quirks. The Kitchen Lead knows the supplier's personal mobile number. The closing Shift Lead knows the alarm code sequence. If they leave, the knowledge leaves.
**How to prevent:** Section 2.5 specifically checks for this. For every "bus factor = 1" item, create a written procedure or share the knowledge with at least one other person.
**If it is already happening:** Sit down with the key person. Ask them to walk through the process while you write it down. Turn it into a document. File it. Brief one other person.

### Risk 3: Audit Is Quarterly But Changes Happen Daily

**Why it happens:** The quarterly cycle means 3 months of drift between audits. Recipes change, suppliers switch, new equipment arrives, and the manual falls behind.
**How to prevent:** Use T080-04 Change Log as a living document. Any time a change is made, log it immediately. The quarterly audit then becomes verification, not discovery.
**If it is already happening:** Add "Update the Change Log" to the closing procedure for any day when something changed. Train Shift Leads to flag changes.

---

## Customisation Notes

**This tool is a starting point, not a rulebook.**

**Independent / Specialty Cafe:** Add a section for coffee programme knowledge (roaster relationship, brewing parameters, seasonal blend changes).
**Chain / Franchise:** Align with your brand's documentation standards. Use this audit to verify local supplements are current alongside corporate materials.
**Kiosk / Mobile:** Simplify to Sections 1 and 2 only. Fewer documents, but they still need to be current.
**Bakery Cafe:** Add bakery recipe documentation, proving schedules, and supplier spec sheets for specialty flours/ingredients.

**Making it yours:**
- Schedule the audit in your calendar now for all 4 quarters
- Involve the team: ask 2 different staff members the usability questions each quarter
- Keep an "update needed" sticky note pad near the manual -- anyone can flag an issue anytime
- Celebrate a clean audit with the team -- it means the system is working

The structure matters more than the specific fields. Keep the structure, change the content.

---

## Connected Resources

**Other tools in this block:**
- T080-01 -- Knowledge Capture Worksheet (L1, template)
- T080-02 -- Standard Document Template (L2, template)
- T080-03 -- Operations Manual Index (L2, template)
- T080-04 -- Change Log Tracker (L2, tracker)

**Related blocks:**
- B077
- B078
- B079

**Parent block:** B080 -- Knowledge Capture System
**Module:** M04: Recruitment Onboarding & Development
---

*Tool Version 1.0 -- OIS Cafe v2*
*Built for cafe operators who value clarity, consistency, and craft.*
